info.name: Bifrost Model Catalog Wiring
info.description: End-to-end wiring tests between the management API and the model catalog. Each scenario stands up an isolated, run-namespaced custom provider backed by a real upstream (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini), mutates its providers/keys, and asserts the catalog read endpoints reflect each mutation. Reads that depend on the asynchronously populated live-model cache poll with exponential backoff. Machine-generated by runners/build-model-catalog-wiring.mjs — do not hand-edit.

item.name: Add provider and key surfaces models (openai)
item.description: A fresh provider plus one gated key surfaces that key's allowed model in the catalog.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (expectEmpty && names.length !== 0) { throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [openai-add-provider-and-key]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [openai-add-provider-and-key]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Alias resolves to underlying model at inference (openai)
item.description: A key alias routes an inference request to the underlying wire model rather than being rejected.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (expectEmpty && names.length !== 0) { throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('inference status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }
if (!body.choices || body.choices.length === 0) { throw new Error('inference returned no choices'); }
if (used.indexOf("gpt-4o-mini") < 0) { throw new Error('expected resolved model gpt-4o-mini in response.model=' + used); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Deleting one key leaves sibling models intact (gemini)
item.description: With two keys gating distinct models, deleting one removes only its models; the sibling's survive.

item.name: Alias resolves to underlying model at inference (gemini)
item.description: A key alias routes an inference request to the underlying wire model rather than being rejected.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (expectEmpty && names.length !== 0) { throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }

if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }

var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [gemini-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('inference status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }
if (!body.choices || body.choices.length === 0) { throw new Error('inference returned no choices'); }
if (used.indexOf("gemini-2.5-flash-lite") < 0) { throw new Error('expected resolved model gemini-2.5-flash-lite in response.model=' + used); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [gemini-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
